args.query: site:ncdhhs.gov vital records birth certificate order online North Carolina

content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: North Carolina Birth Certificates\
  \ | North Carolina Death Certificates | GoCertificates.com\n- url: https://www.gocertificates.com/new/NorthCarolina.aspx\n\
  - source: GoCertificates.com\n- domain: gocertificates.com\n\n## Content\n\n# Order\
  \ Certified North Carolina Birth and Death Certificates Online\n\nGoCertificates\
  \ presents its North Carolina page as an online resource for ordering certified\
  \ copies of State of North Carolina vital records. The page highlights \u201CSave\
  \ Time and Money,\u201D \u201CFast and Easy Ordering,\u201D and \u201CNo Waiting\
  \ in Line.\u201D It states that GoCertificates is committed to getting birth or\
  \ death certificates direct to the requester\u2019s door.\n\nThe page states that\
  \ requests for vital events certificates are directed to and processed by the North\
  \ Carolina Department of Health and Human Services. It provides links or buttons\
  \ for ordering birth certificates, ordering death certificates, and ordering a name\
  \ change kit.\n\n## Processing Times\n\nThe page lists current processing time for\
  \ North Carolina as 30 to 90 business days. Processing begins once the requester\
  \ has submitted all required documents through GoCertificates.com. Shipping time\
  \ is separate from processing time, and the requester should add both together for\
  \ the total expected delivery window. The page states that processing time is determined\
  \ by North Carolina Vital Records, not GoCertificates, and that times may vary based\
  \ on current volume.\n\n## Identification Requirements\n\nThe page instructs applicants\
  \ to submit a copy of an accepted form of identification and proof of relationship\
  \ if required. After placing an order, the applicant must upload an image of identification\
  \ and proof of relationship if required. The page says an order will only be processed\
  \ once a legible copy of identification and proof of relationship, if required,\
  \ are received along with payment.\n\nAccepted current photo identification listed\
  \ on the page includes a current state-issued driver\u2019s license, current state-issued\
  \ non-driver\u2019s license, current passport or visa with photo, current U.S. military\
  \ ID, Department of Corrections photo ID dated within the last year, current state\
  \ or U.S. government agency photo ID for persons requesting certificates as part\
  \ of that agency\u2019s business, and current student ID with copy of transcript.\n\
  \nIf the applicant cannot provide one of the listed photo IDs, the page says the\
  \ applicant must submit at least two secondary forms of ID from a list. Examples\
  \ include a temporary driver\u2019s license, current utility bill with current address,\
  \ car registration or title with current address, bank statement with current address,\
  \ pay stub with current address, income tax return or W-2 showing current address,\
  \ a letter from a government agency dated within the last six months showing current\
  \ address, and a state-issued concealed weapon permit showing current address.\n\
  \n## Who Can Order\n\nFor birth certificates, the page lists categories such as\
  \ self, spouse, brother, sister, child, parent, step-parent, grandparent, authorized\
  \ agent, and legal representative. Some categories require valid photo identification,\
  \ and authorized agents or legal representatives may require supporting documents.\
  \ For death certificates, the page lists similar categories and document requirements.\n\
  \n## Questions About an Order\n\nThe page provides a customer support phone number\
  \ and an email address for questions about North Carolina orders, along with weekday\
  \ business hours.\n\nThe footer states that GoCertificates has been a trusted source\
  \ for ordering certified vital records online since 2007. The legal notice at the\
  \ bottom says GoCertificates.com, Inc. is a privately owned company that provides\
  \ the service of expediting vital records ordering. It says GoCertificates is not\
  \ a government agency and that vital certificates and forms may be ordered from\
  \ the relevant government agency for free or a lesser cost. The fee covers a secure\
  \ online ordering service, expert assistance, and review of information.\n\n"
metadata: {}
title: North Carolina Birth Certificates | North Carolina Death Certificates | GoCertificates.com
url: https://www.gocertificates.com/new/NorthCarolina.aspx

args.query: North Carolina Vital Records online ordering vendor authorized birth certificate fee

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: NC Vital Records: Order a Certificate

  - url: https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/order.htm

  - source: North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Public
  Health, NC Office of Vital Records

  - domain: vitalrecords.nc.gov


  ## Content


  # NC Vital Records: Order a Certificate


  ## General Issuance Certificate Ordering Options


  Step through the ordering questions and routing to guide you to the correct information
  and ordering resources for the certificate service you need to request. Before scheduling
  an appointment or placing your certificate order, check NC Vital Records processing
  dates for estimated processing and shipping dates to determine if the timeframes
  meet your needs.


  If the NC Vital Records timeframes do not meet your needs, birth, death, and marriage
  certificates may also be ordered from the Register of Deeds in the county of the
  event. Divorce certificates may also be obtained from the Clerk of Court in the
  county where the divorce is filed.


  ## Start an Order: Preliminary Questions and Routing


  The ordering workflow asks what type of certificate is needed: birth, death, marriage,
  divorce, or fetal death. Some requests are not regular certificate orders. For example,
  a new birth certificate following an adoption must use the Adoptions process and
  forms. NC Vital Records states: Important: Do not place new adoption orders through
  VitalChek.


  Requests to add a father, amend or correct a birth or death certificate, or make
  other certificate changes are routed to the Changes/Amendments page or the relevant
  paternity process. Marriage certificate changes or replacements must be handled
  by the county Register of Deeds where the marriage license was issued. Divorce certificate
  changes must be handled by the county Clerk of Court where the divorce was filed.


  ## How Do You Want to Order?


  The page lists ordering options including online, by phone, in person, by mail,
  local Register of Deeds, and Clerk of Court.


  ### Online Orders through VitalChek


  Use the online order link to order through VitalChek. NC Vital Records identifies
  VitalChek as the only authorized vendor for a state-issued, certified birth certificate.
  VitalChek is an independent service. VitalChek charges a processing fee, and major
  credit cards are accepted, including American Express, Discover, MasterCard, and
  VISA. VitalChek also accepts personal checks.


  Applicants ordering through VitalChek will be asked to provide a credit card number
  and expiration date, provide the necessary certificate information, and provide
  a street address where the certificate can be delivered. APO, FPO, and PO Boxes
  are not accepted for delivery. If the UPS overnight option is chosen, someone must
  be available at the delivery address to sign for receipt of the documents.


  ### Online and Phone Order Processing Fees


  The NC State search fee includes one certificate if the record is located. Search
  fees and expedite fees are nonrefundable even if a record is not located when searching
  based on the information provided by the applicant. The listed online and phone
  ordering costs include the NC State search fee, VitalChek processing fee, regular
  USPS shipping at no charge, and optional overnight UPS shipping within the continental
  United States.


  ### Phone Orders through VitalChek


  Phone orders are also handled through VitalChek, the only authorized vendor for
  a state-issued, certified birth certificate. NC Vital Records lists the toll-free
  phone number for VitalChek and notes that VitalChek is the only authorized vendor
  for that service.


  ### In-Person, Mail, Register of Deeds, and Clerk of Court


  In-person certificate service is available by appointment only. Customer service
  appointment hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., and the last
  available appointment is 2:30 p.m. In-person expedited processing fees apply in
  addition to standard fees.


  Mail order applications should be sent to the attention of VitalChek with payment
  made to VitalChek. The mail order instructions say to complete, print, and sign
  the appropriate form; include the first-copy fee, additional-copy fees, and the
  VitalChek processing fee noted on the application; make payment to VitalChek; do
  not send cash; and do not send an expedited fee.


  Birth, death, and marriage certificates may also be obtained at the Register of
  Deeds office in the county where the event took place. In most cases, certificates
  can be obtained from the Register of Deeds on the same day. Divorce certificates
  may also be obtained from the Clerk of Court in the county where the divorce is
  filed.


  ## Place an Order: Overview


  Records available through NCOVR include birth certificates from 1913 to present,
  death certificates from 1930 to present, marriage certificates from 1962 to present,
  divorce certificates from 1958 to present, and fetal death reports from 2001 to
  present. Non-standard orders, including amendments, corrections, paternity matters,
  and certificates after adoption, are routed to separate pages and processes.


  The payment processing section notes that orders placed through NC Vital Records
  include a nonrefundable search fee per three-year period, even if a record is not
  found. Due to order volume and system migrations, there is usually a lag time between
  payment processing and order completion or delivery. If a record is not found, NC
  Vital Records will contact the requester. Payments above the search fee can be refunded
  when a record is not found and an order is cancelled.


  '
metadata: {}
title: 'NC Vital Records: Order a Certificate'
url: https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/order.htm

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: NC Vital Records: Fees and Payment\n\
  - url: https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/fees.htm\n- source: North Carolina Department\
  \ of Health and Human Services, Division of Public Health, NC Office of Vital Records\n\
  - domain: vitalrecords.nc.gov\n\n## Content\n\n# NC Vital Records: Fees and Payment\n\
  \nNorth Carolina law requires payment before a certificate search or a service can\
  \ be provided. Fees are not refundable after a search or service has been provided.\
  \ Certificates are sent first class mail unless otherwise requested and additional\
  \ fees are applied. When ordering through NC Vital Records, the fee schedule on\
  \ this page applies.\n\n## Payment Processing and Order Delivery\n\nThere is a $24\
  \ search fee, which includes one copy if the record is located, for each three-year\
  \ period or portion of a three-year period searched. The search fee is nonrefundable,\
  \ even if a record is not found, so NC Vital Records must process payments before\
  \ completing the order.\n\nDue to order volume and system migrations from paper\
  \ to electronic, there is usually a lag time between payment processing and order\
  \ completion and delivery. This delay can be longer if an order has issues that\
  \ must be researched or addressed, such as amendments to certificates. If a record\
  \ is not found during the search, NC Vital Records will contact the requester. Payments\
  \ above the $24 search fee can be refunded when a record is not found and an order\
  \ is cancelled. Requesters should consult the current processing times for certificate\
  \ and order types.\n\n## Methods of Payment\n\nFees may be paid in different ways\
  \ depending on the order method.\n\nFor mailed-in orders, payment may be made by\
  \ money order, certified check, or business check. Cash is not accepted through\
  \ the mail. Personal checks are not accepted through the mail. All certified checks,\
  \ business checks, and money orders should be made payable to \u201CNC Vital Records.\u201D\
  \n\nFor online or phone orders, payment is made by personal check, credit card,\
  \ or debit card through VitalChek. For in-person orders, NC Vital Records accepts\
  \ major credit cards and electronic check payments. Cash is accepted for in-person\
  \ services only. Cash should not be sent in the mail. In-person certificate service\
  \ is available by appointment only, and details are available on the Order page.\
  \ All checks and money orders should be made payable to \u201CNC Vital Records.\u201D\
  \n\n## Fee Schedule\n\nThe standard certificate search fee for one three-year period\
  \ includes one copy of the certificate if a record is found. The search fee applies\
  \ to any certificate type and is nonrefundable, even if a record is not found. If\
  \ no record is found, NC Vital Records sends a letter.\n\nAdditional searches cost\
  \ $24 for each additional three-year period or portion of a three-year period. Additional\
  \ copies of the same certificate cost $15 each.\n\nThe VitalChek processing fee\
  \ is $13.95. This fee is nonrefundable if the requester orders through VitalChek.\
  \ The page states that VitalChek is the only authorized vendor through which constituents\
  \ may submit a remote order for a state-issued, certified birth certificate. VitalChek\
  \ charges a processing fee, and major credit cards are accepted, including American\
  \ Express, Discover, MasterCard, and VISA. VitalChek also accepts personal checks.\n\
  \nOther service fees listed on the schedule include amending birth or death certificates\
  \ or fetal death reports, birth certificates after adoption, court-ordered name\
  \ changes, legitimations, paternities, and expedited processing. Expedited processing\
  \ fees are nonrefundable even if a record is not located. Expedited in-person service\
  \ is available by appointment only. Alternatively, birth, death, or marriage certificates\
  \ may be ordered from the Register of Deeds.\n\nThere is no charge for regular shipping.\
  \ Overnight shipping costs vary.\n\n"
metadata: {}
title: 'NC Vital Records: Fees and Payment'
url: https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/fees.htm

You likely **do not need to complete that “information review” as a government-required step**.

What I found:

- **GoCertificates.com identifies itself as a privately owned expediting service, not a government agency.** Its page says its fee includes “review of information.”
- The **North Carolina Office of Vital Records** says that **VitalChek is the only authorized vendor for remote online or phone orders** for a state-issued, certified birth certificate.
- North Carolina’s official ordering page does **not** list GoCertificates as an authorized vendor or require a preliminary review before ordering.

### What you should do

1. **Do not upload identification or pay GoCertificates yet** if you have not already done so.
2. Start from North Carolina’s official page:  
   https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/order.htm
3. For a standard online order, use the VitalChek link provided there. You may also be able to order through the county **Register of Deeds** where the birth occurred.
4. Have the usual identification and eligibility documents ready. A special process may apply if this is an adoption-related certificate, an amendment/correction, or another nonstandard request.

The “information review” may be an optional service offered by GoCertificates, but based on the official North Carolina guidance, it is **not a prerequisite imposed by North Carolina**.

final_answer:
You likely **do not need to complete that “information review” as a government-required step**.

What I found:

- **GoCertificates.com identifies itself as a privately owned expediting service, not a government agency.** Its page says its fee includes “review of information.”
- The **North Carolina Office of Vital Records** says that **VitalChek is the only authorized vendor for remote online or phone orders** for a state-issued, certified birth certificate.
- North Carolina’s official ordering page does **not** list GoCertificates as an authorized vendor or require a preliminary review before ordering.

### What you should do

1. **Do not upload identification or pay GoCertificates yet** if you have not already done so.
2. Start from North Carolina’s official page:  
   https://vitalrecords.nc.gov/order.htm
3. For a standard online order, use the VitalChek link provided there. You may also be able to order through the county **Register of Deeds** where the birth occurred.
4. Have the usual identification and eligibility documents ready. A special process may apply if this is an adoption-related certificate, an amendment/correction, or another nonstandard request.

The “information review” may be an optional service offered by GoCertificates, but based on the official North Carolina guidance, it is **not a prerequisite imposed by North Carolina**.
